closed_today.reason: Crude reversal post-Mideast de-escalation; pre-empted SL trip
TRAIL-GAP VIOLATION — stored SL 207.78 above CMP 206.90, forced exit
Apr 2026 production -9.7% YoY, fundamental deterioration on top of 52w high
Binary-event panic exit at -1% (Rule 15 violation — pre-existing event was known at entry)
Trail-gap violation + Q4 May 19 binary; clean +5% lock-in
TRAIL-GAP VIOLATION — stored SL 950 above CMP 949.55, forced exit at marginal gain
SL hit at -2.6% on event-trade into May 6 board meeting

new_entries_open.note: Same-day re-entry 90 min after exit; Rule 14 cooldown borderline

patterns_found: PATTERN 1 — Trail-gap discipline failed twice on the same playbook: BANDHANBNK (SL trailed 200→207.78 with CMP ~207, gap ~0.4%) and BAJFINANCE (SL trailed 944→950 with CMP 949.55, gap ~0.05%) both forced same-session 'SL-above-CMP' exits. Wins were lucky outcomes (+₹2,878 and +₹199); the trail itself caused both exits, not the chart. Existing 0.5% rule was tighter than NSE's typical intraday noise band.

PATTERN 2 — Event-trade entries lost 2-for-2: BAJAJ-AUTO (conf 0.62, May 6 board, 2 sessions out) -₹570 in 62 minutes; EXIDEIND (conf 0.68, May 6 board, 2 sessions out) -₹1,434 in 90 minutes. Combined -₹2,004. Both at sub-0.70 conviction, both into the SAME May 6 calendar. Auto-sector binary risk fired the same tail twice within 3 hours.

PATTERN 3 — Same-name same-day re-entry: BANDHANBNK SELL 211.51 at 10:15 → BUY 209 at 11:45 (90 min later). Rule 14 mandates 5-session cooldown unless new catalyst or invalidated thesis; bot's reasoning didn't acknowledge prior exit. Root cause is upstream: the 10:15 exit was a forced trail-gap exit, not thesis-driven. Fix the trail, the re-entry disappears.

PATTERN 4 — Risk overrides were non-binding all session: max_sector_pct cap 0.28 vs largest sector BFSI 12.88% (15+ pp buffer); max_deployed_pct cap 0.75 vs deployment 46.73% (28+ pp buffer); min_confidence 0.60 vs strategy's organic 0.62-0.70 floor. Strategy is self-disciplined; non-binding overrides only generate drift between system_prompt placeholders and actual enforcement.

PATTERN 5 — Win/loss split by category: BFSI +₹3,076 (2W/0L), Pharma +₹2,591 (1W/0L), Coal/Mining +₹1,276 (1W/0L) — all winners on legacy positions exited on thesis or trail-gap mechanics. Auto -₹2,003 (0W/2L) — both losses were event-trades. Oil&Gas -₹1,427 (0W/1L) — pre-empted SL on macro reversal. Loss cluster is event-trades, not sectoral.

PATTERN 6 — Activity level was healthy: 14 trade events (6 new, 7 closes, 1 same-name re-entry), 0 NO_ACTION-only ticks during market hours. Loosened-overrides regime worked — bot did not sit out cycles, deployed capital actively, captured rotation between exits and new admissions. Activity level is no longer a problem.

PATTERN 7 — Guardrail block noise: 4 'Confidence 0.0 below 0.6 threshold' blocks (default-conf NO_ACTION proposals routed through validator), 4 'Cannot short-sell in CNC' blocks (duplicate close attempts after position already exited), 1 'MODIFY for X but no open position' block (same root cause). All are engine-side noise, not real strategy errors. None should drive parameter changes.

guardrail_effectiveness.comment: All blocks were noise — no strategy proposals were materially declined by guardrails. The min_confidence override at 0.60 never fired against a real signal. Override regime was effectively decorative — strategy was already operating in the conservative envelope on its own.

deployment_assessment.comment: Below the 50% target despite high activity. Cash buffer is generous. After reverting deployed-cap override to base 0.80, strategy has 33+ pp of headroom for higher conviction days. No urgent action needed; let next session evidence drive whether to push deployment higher.

changes_made.what: Added: event-trade entries require min confidence ≥ 0.70 AND at most ONE event-trade per session. Cited Day-10 failure (BAJAJ-AUTO + EXIDEIND -₹2,004 combined).
changes_made.why: Two losing event-trade entries today, both at sub-0.70 conviction, both into the same May 6 calendar. Existing rule allowed event-trades but had no conviction floor or per-session cap.
changes_made.expected_impact: Suppresses sub-0.70 binary-event entries (would have blocked BAJAJ-AUTO at conf 0.62), prevents stacking event-trades on the same calendar (would have blocked the second of two). Does not affect non-event swing entries.

changes_made.what: Tightened required trail-gap from 0.5% → 1.0% below LTP for longs (1.0% above for shorts). Added Day-10 failure modes (BANDHANBNK and BAJFINANCE) as examples.
changes_made.why: Two forced exits today on 'SL above CMP' pattern — strategy trailed SL into <0.5% gap from CMP, then exited when normal noise pushed CMP onto SL. Win outcomes obscured the design failure.
changes_made.expected_impact: Stops trailed SLs from sitting in the intraday noise band. Positions get room to run to real targets. Same-name same-day re-entries (today: BANDHANBNK round-trip) should disappear since the upstream cause is removed.

changes_made.what: Removed max_sector_pct override (reverts to base 0.35 from 0.28). Removed max_deployed_pct override (reverts to base 0.80 from 0.75). Loosened min_confidence override 0.60 → 0.55. Kept max_position_pct 0.15, default_sl_pct 0.015, min_sl_pct 0.01, max_sl_pct 0.045.
changes_made.why: All three reverted/loosened overrides were non-binding throughout the session. Original justifications (early-week cluster-stack incidents, MAHABANK SL blow-up) have not recurred. Reverting reduces system_prompt-vs-guardrail drift and gives more headroom. Kept SL-width and size overrides because today's event-trade losses validate keeping per-name risk tight.
changes_made.expected_impact: Hot-effect immediate. Strategy gets sector-cap headroom, deployment headroom, and one more notch of low-conviction signal admission. Should raise activity slightly without raising per-trade risk (size/SL caps unchanged).

recommendations: Re-evaluate the same-name re-entry rule (Rule 14): in cases where the prior exit was a mechanical trail-gap exit (not a thesis exit), the cooldown is questionable. Today's BANDHANBNK round-trip was technically a violation but produced acceptable outcome. Consider clarifying that trail-gap-induced exits don't trigger the cooldown, OR (better) prevent the trail-gap exit upstream — which today's edits address.

Investigate engine-side noise blocks: 4 duplicate close attempts after position-already-exited and 4 zero-confidence routing blocks suggest the strategy layer is re-issuing close orders for positions it doesn't realize are gone, or routing NO_ACTION through the validator with default 0.0 confidence. Engine-side fix: reject 0.0-confidence NO_ACTION before guardrail; on close orders, re-read paper_holdings before submission.

Monitor the new event-trade conviction floor across the next 3-5 sessions. If it suppresses too many entries (e.g., legitimate 0.65-0.69 setups into 4-session-out events), consider lowering the floor to 0.65 with the per-session cap kept at 1.

Day-10 was a healthy active-trader day: 14 events, +0.35% return, no NO_ACTION cycles, 47% deployment. The loosened-overrides regime is producing data worth analyzing. Continue this direction — do NOT tighten overrides reflexively on the two event-trade losses; they were a discipline failure (entering at sub-0.70 conf into binary calendars), not a system-risk failure.

Consider a future change: bump max_position_pct override 0.15 → 0.18 (still below base 0.20) once the bot demonstrates one full session of conviction-scaled sizing where a 0.70+ entry is sized at 8-10%. Currently all entries are clustered at 5% regardless of conviction — the 'scale size with conviction' rule isn't biting because the position cap is too uniform.
